statement: The Althingi (Icelandic parliament) has 63 seats. As of session 157, 57 seats are held by sitting MPs. The party breakdown is: Sjálfstæðisflokkur (Independence Party) 13, Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) 13, Viðreisn (Reform Party) 11, Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party) 8, Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) 8, Framsóknarflokkur (Progressive Party) 4. The remaining 6 seats are vacant or held by alternates (varamenn).
caveats: Seat counts reflect session 157 and may change due to resignations, by-elections, or party switches. Varamenn (alternates) serve when sitting MPs take leave.

related_entries.statement: Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) and Viðreisn (Reform Party) are the two Althingi parties that formally support EU membership. Samfylkingin has supported EU membership since 2007 and led the 2009 application process while in government. Viðreisn was founded in 2016 partly on a pro-EU platform and has consistently advocated for completing accession negotiations. Together they held approximately 17 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party), Framsóknarflokkurinn (Progressive Party), Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) oppose EU membership. The Independence Party has historically been the strongest opponent, citing sovereignty and fisheries concerns. The Progressive Party, traditionally representing rural interests, opposes membership primarily over agricultural and fisheries policy. These four parties collectively held approximately 33–35 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

The current Icelandic government is a coalition of Samfylkingin (Social Democrats, 13 seats), Viðreisn (Reform Party, 11 seats), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party, 8 seats), holding 32 of 63 Althingi seats. The Prime Minister is Kristrún Frostadóttir (Samfylkingin). This is the first government coalition where both explicitly pro-EU parties (Samfylkingin and Viðreisn) serve together, which has been cited as a factor in advancing the EU referendum.
